violations.text: Active managerial control is needed and continued staff training. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
The hand-washing sink in the duck prep room was lacking paper towels. Paper towels provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
Inspector observed avocados, located in the produce walk-in cooler, visibly gnawed. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
Trapped roaches observed on glue board in home goods aisle. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
The refuse and loading areas have an accumulation of pallets and food debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026

The ceiling above the produce walk-in cooler is in poor repair. Inspector observed opening around piping. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The hand-washing sink in the seafood department was blocked by a dunnage rack and cases of fish. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Items removed and relocated to walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Several boxes of food were observed stored on the floor in the retail dry goods aisle(s). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC instructed to relocate food boxes. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The refuse area has a heavy accumulation of pallets and unused equipment. Area to be cleaned and maintained. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The wall beneath the band saw (meat retail area) is in poor repair. Opening observed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Gloves were observed in the (seafood) hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Hand sink cleared during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

The presence of rodents and roaches are not controlled as evidenced by gnawing, structural damage and live activity. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Wood is used in the construction of soap holders in the seafood/meat areas. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026

The condensate from the ice machine unit is draining onto the floor. Standing water observed. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2026
The refuse and loading areas area have a heavy accumulation of pallets, wooden baskets, food debris and clutter. Areas are to be cleaned and maintained. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The walls in the retail band saw area and warehouse near kitchen door are in poor repair. Large openings and structural damage observed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/24/2026
The hand-washing sink in the meat retail area was lacking paper towels. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Gizzards, pig ears, tripe, cabbage were observed held at 92-113 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026
Roach activity observed in dry food storage areas outside of office. Active treatment and monitoring to continue. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Wood is used in the construction of food storage table and soap holders for the (meat/seafood) hand sink areas. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2026
The outdoor refuse and areas of parking lot have an accumulation of cardboard boxes and pallets. PIC has contacted refuse Co. for pickup. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The floors throughout the warehouse/storage area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Frozen crickets and sandworms (specifically labeled keep frozen) were observed held at 39 degrees Fahrenheit and thawed. Disposed. PIC informed to follow temperature guidelines by manufacturer. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2025
Cold well freezers located in the retail area was not in good repair. (Ambient 32-35 degrees F.) Service technicians on-site for repair work. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/06/2025
The outdoor refuse area and loading area has a heavy accumulation of pallets and clutter. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

Tripe was observed thawing in stagnant water (3 bay sink). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Tripe was fully thawed and relocated to the walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings, gnawing and structural damage. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelves and food storage containers throughout the kitchen have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025
The refuse area has a large accumulation of wooden pallets, baskets and unnecessary items (truck tire, etc.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The floor beneath the wok station and oil disposal tank area has an accumulation of rodent droppings, soil residue and/or other debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

Inspector observed a leak coming from the wok station and dripping onto the floor. Large openings observed near hot water tank, wall in storage area and wall/roof area near Kimchi cooler. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

Molluscan Shellfish (clams) located in (retail seafood display) did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

The presence of rodent droppings are not controlled as evidenced by (rodent droppings) in the boiler room. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Cases of produce were observed stored on the floor in the retail produce area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC instructed employee to relocated produce during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the counters, storage shelves, food containers in the kitchen (cook line) and the table (duck prep room) have an accumulation of food, soil residue and grease. The employee coffee table is constructed of wood. This material is not cleanable, durable and is porous. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025
The plumbing to the 3-bay sink (meat department) and hand sink (retail seafood area) are in poor repair. Leak observed and water unavailable. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025
The refuse area for the oil barrel has a heavy accumulation of oil, leaves and organic matter. PIC informed area must be scraped, cleaned and maintained. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025

The wall in the boiler room and retail seafood area is in poor repair. Inspector observed an opening above electrical outlet plate and an opening to wall (proper material is needed). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2025

The floors in the duck prep room and the parking lot have an accumulation of grease build up, soil residue, food debris and pallets. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2025
(Various food product ) were observed stored on the floor in the (walk in cooler #1 and #2 ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the (prep table shelves, food storage bins(white), and counter under bread cutting machine ) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
3 bay sink is in poor repair.Observed leak at the plumbing. Per PIC, service call was made. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment, items, and pallets were observed in the outside and throughout the establishment area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

(Chicharron and poultry wings ) was observed held at (80-87) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Meat reheated to 165 and above. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The presence of (rodents) is not controlled as evidenced by (droppings throughout facility). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
Inspector observed dead rodent on trap.PIC removed from premise. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
Inspector observed meat being stored uncovered in meat freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
(Various food product ) were observed stored on the floor in the (freezers and produce areas ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the (prep table shelves, open case retail shelves, food storage bins(white), and bread cutting machine table) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The outdoor grease trap area observed with an accumulation of trash, litter, and grease. Refuse areas must be kept clean as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the (outside and throughout establishment ) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
The (walls ) in the (above oven) bakery area is in poor repair.Observed a hole The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
Various frozen items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer (#2). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the mixer shield have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
Pan were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Inspector observed adulterated(mold) in packages of cheese.PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine and floor mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch.Ice machine taken out of service and ice voluntarily disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

(Various food product) was observed held at ( 117-126 ) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated food product during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
(Food product ) were observed stored on the floor in the (walk in freezers ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the (food storage shelves in kitchen, walk in coolers, under hot hold area, food storage bins, stove hoods ) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2025
Insoector observed a leak on sprinkler head in ceiling. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/21/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the (kitchen, sprinkler room, seafood dept, and parking lot) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The floors under seafood filters and bakery have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the (food storage ) area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Sponge and brush were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024

The door to the outside, located in the (side door near seafood department), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Meat in walk in freezers observed being stored uncovered. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024
The hot water to the (BBQ) hand sinks is lacking adequate pressure. Per PIC, ordered part and waiting to receive. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024
Inspector observed a heavy accumulation of pallets being stored in rear parking lot of establishment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

The outside refuse area observed with an accumulation of boxes, trash, food debris, and/or other debris. Outside refuse areas must be kept clean as often as necessary to keep them clean. Per PIC, trash pickup is scheduled for pickup on 10/8/24. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2024
The (walls) in the (seafood) area is in poor repair. Observed holes The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

The walls in bakery and floors in sprinkler room have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Hammer and sponge were observed in the hand-washing sink in seafood department. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Packages of flour and instant noodles observed with gnaw marks. PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. See disposal Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC informed. Unit take out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Thermometer for meat display coolers observed display inaccurate ambient temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

The presence of (rodents) are not controlled as evidenced by (droppings) in several areas of establishment. Dead mouse was observed on trap in bakery area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

The door to the outside, located in the (side door), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
Meat in the walk in coolers and freezers was observed being stored uncovered in walk in cooler and freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Inspector observed unclean hooks being stored on a trash barrel. Employee removed hooks to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Wood is used in the construction of (food prep table ) in the (meat department) area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces observed with debris: 1.exterior of ice machine 2.food storage shelves in kitchen observed with food residue and old droppings 3.retail food storage shelves(instant noodle) have debris. 4.employee break area observed with storage shelves. 5. food storage buckets/bins observed with soil and food residue. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024
The hot water to the (BBQ) handsink is lacking adequate pressure. New Violation. Correct By: 09/23/2024
Inspector observed a heavy accumulation of pallets being stored in rear parking lot of establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the (kitchen and seafood department ) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

The (walls) in the (bakery and dry food storage area) area is in poor repair. Observed a hole in wall in the bakery behind 2 bay sink and back wall in storage area. The floor in walk in coolerA16 observed lifting. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

The floors in the kitchen, back storage, walk in coolers, break rooms, and produce storage area. The walls in the bakery and walk in cooler. have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
Employee break room is need of a thorough cleaning. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

Beef and Turkey organs were observed held at (116 and 117) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
Hot holding unit was observed in poor repair. Water leaking from basin. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
The following was observed in poor repair: 1. Leaks observed at 3 bay sinks at bakery, large cooking room, and seafood area 2. Prep sink at produce area Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024
The ceiling tiles in back houseware area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bakery kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Plastic on shrimp paste was observed gnawed. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine in seafood area) were observed with an accumulation of black debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service during time of inspection. Ice machine must be cleaned and sanitized prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Cooked Vegetables were observed held at (118) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Due to quality issues per PIC, food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a live rodent observed during time of inspection and adulterated food. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of bakery dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Round wooden blocks were observed being used as cutting and preparation surfaces in the BBQ area. Items were taken out of service and voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

The cutting boards in the seafood area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Greasetrap in large back kitchen is lacking grease pan. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: 1. bakery mixers 2. exterior of icemachines 3. dunnage and hanging racks in WI Cooler #1 New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

The following was observed in poor repair: 1. Leaks observed at 3 bay sinks at bakery, large cooking room, back meat department (at bottom pipes) and seafood area (at faucet) 2. prep sink at produce area New Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024
The ceiling tiles in back houseware area was observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/29/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2024

The hand-washing sink in the bakery area was blocked by a mixing paddle. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2024
Greasetrap in large back kitchen is lacking grease pan. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2024
Gaskets for walk-in cooler in meat area were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2024

The wall by meatcutting area prep table is constructed of painted sheet rock. This material is not durable. Materials for wall surfaces shall be smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2024
The ceiling tiles in back houseware area was observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in cooler #6. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Beverage spilled on prep table during inspection. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the seafood area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Employee was also observed washing hands in the 2 bay sink in bakery room. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024
1 case of white glutinous flour in the back stockroom and tapioca starch in aisle 8 was observed gnawed. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (strainer and cooking utensils in BBQ room) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024
Insecticide was observed in the bakery area. PIC informed to remove from premises. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by adulterated food and rodent droppings in back room. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024
Greasetrap in large back kitchen is lacking grease pan. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2024
Gaskets for walk-in cooler in meat area were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. 1. 2 bay sink in bakery room 2. wall near packaging machine in meat area 3. BBQ room walls New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2024

The following was observed in poor repair: 1. Roof in back storeroom (starting from middle seam of store) 2. Ceiling tiles in back houseware area 3. Produce walk-in cooler seams and holes The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
Floors in back storage room was observed with water. Physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/10/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in cooler #6. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2024

(WD 40) was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023

Working containers of soju sake alcohol (in spray bottle) was not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2023
Proper air gap lacking at 2 bay food prep sink in BBQ room (sink has an air break). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed at the exterior refuse area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2023
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023
2 fish were observed deceased in water holding tank. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
Fresh shellstock were observed open and did not close to touch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
Packaged beef neck bone was observed unwholesome to sight and smell. Foods were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023

Food in the retail open air case was observed held at 46-57 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time was voluntarily disposed of by the manager. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Frozen ready to eat desserts, were observed held at refrigerated temperatures. Per label, product is to be labelled at retail facility post defrost. Per interview of manager, previously frozen desserts were placed in open air case on Thursday. Market to follow manufacturer's parameters for holding and date marking product post defrost. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Fresh rice noodles packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Product removed from sale at time of inspection. Product cannot be placed for self-service sale without proper labelling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the open retail freezer cases have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the both produce walk-in coolers. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

The door and gate to the outside, located by the BBQ room and loading dock gate, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

1. Faucet at exterior refuse area observed in poor repair 2. Air gap is needed for BBQ Room prep sink 3. Drains for BBQ room and bakery observed in poor repair Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/07/2023

Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. The person in charge must demonstrate to the health department knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this Code. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023
Bags of rice were observed gnawed in pallets located in back storage room. Foods were voluntarily disposed by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

Integrity of rice packaging observed compromised. Inspectors observed taped corners of bagged rice to at least 2 individual units of rice that were previously gnawed. Foods were voluntarily disposed. See attachments. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by adulterated foods and strong scent of urine. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2023
Gnawed foods were observed in noodle aisle and spice aisle. PIC to disclose list of disposed foods. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2023

Working containers of bakery ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses in BBQ serving area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2023

Wood is used in the construction of table in seafood area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the caulking at seafood 3 bay sink have an accumulation of green and pink residue. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelves and curtains in fish walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the white buckets in the bakery area have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of soil residue. New Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the bakery mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the fryer in large kitchen have an accumulation of grease and soil residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

3 bay sinks at meat/seafood area was observed leaking. Faucet at kitchen (3 bay) was observed leaking. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023
Hole observed in bakery area by ovens. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

Stagnant water was observed overflowing from container from grease trap in large kitchen. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/04/2023
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

An open employees' beverage containers was observed stored on cardboard boxes of food in the storage room and in the produce preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
Jumbo Conch was observed with cracked shell. Apparent unwholesome smell was present. Food was voluntarily disposed of by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
Box of noodles observed gnawed. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as lunchables, bread, and figs) in the retail open air cases. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives in produce room) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. (If in use, utensils must be cleaned every four hours.) New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Cut melon was observed held at 53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was moved to colder refrigeration during time of inspection to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Razor Clams and Jumbo Conch were observed held at 49 and 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Additional ice was added to shellstock during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Pork and Duck for BBQ were observed drying at ambient temperature with air from fans. Per interview of PIC, foods are hung out at ambient temperature for 8-9 hours. PIC informed to cease process. Food placed in refrigeration during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Raid was observed in establishment. PIC informed pesticides to be applied by certified pest control technician or under the guidance of a pest control technician. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
Frozen fish was observed thawing in stagnant water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Working containers of spices in BBQ prep area are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2023

The doors to the outside, located at: 1. Produce area 2. Canned beverages in stock room, 3. Loading dock gate 4. Back door by BBQ room, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2023

The presence of small and large rodents is not controlled as evidenced by the strong odor of urine in the backstock room, adulterated foods in aisle 5 and storage backroom, evidence of nesting materials, and small and large rodent droppings. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2023
Food was observed stored on the floor in the produce walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

The cutting blocks in the seafood area are very scratched and scored. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2023

Cardboard was used in the lining of BBQ prep tables in hallway. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the BBQ prep table (bottom shelves, sides and containers) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2023
Air gaps for plumbing at 3 bay sinks in BBQ room and Seafood area are lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2023

Accumulated trash observed at exterior of BBQ room door. Area shall be cleaned as often as necessary in order to prevent attractants and harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

Honeycombs at the open air cases have an accumulation of black residue, dust, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2023

The fans, shelving, and gaskets in the walk-in cooler #1 was observed with dust and black residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed above walk-in freezers and coolers. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2023
The curtains for produce walk-in coolers were observed with black residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/16/2023

Gloves, a sponge, plastic and scrubs were observed in the hand-washing sinks (among seafood area and produce handsink). A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sinks in the seafood and produce areas. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023

Various shellstock was observed held at 46-61 degrees Fahrenheit. Cut pumpkin and wintermelon observed at ambient temperature on display for retail sale. Foods placed in refrigeration to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023

Produce boxes were observed stored on the floor in the retail area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023
